epigraph.quote: The whole building trembles, shudders rhythmically, like being on a ship, but a much faster rhythm.

moments.quote: That night we camped in a very good site in St. Illiers [?en] Ville Domaine de Inchelin
There were hedges between the plots, an oak wood, cages of birds, old barns & a swimming pool.
The floor of the cathedral is paved in lovely old slabs.
There was a midnight swim to celebrate 14th July.
The Loire is very pretty with soft, deciduous, billowing trees.
I could imagine families living there. Tapestries carved fireplaces, warm colours.
The early mist on the water in the morning was beautiful - One of the most lovely views we've had.
Thomas paddled his boat across the lake with Peter in it & William behind, towed on a lilo.
snails, lamb curry, cheese + crêpes. 5 portions served all seven of us.
We picked up food at Tulle, including delicious chocolates — truffles.
they had drained the reservoir behind the dam, so there was just a dirty valley with ghostly dead trees in the bottom.
We stopped at Aurillac & bought Patisseries & changed money.

images.caption: Volcanoes of the Auvergne from west

moments.quote: We have a lovely view looking over the lake, with an artificial swimming pool built on at the far side.
Thomas bought an enormous cow's horn in the shop at the top of the pass.
Simon cooked garlic spaghetti. The boys had to wear swimming hats.
We set off northeastwards, towards Roanne, to interview an au pair,

images.caption: Flying buttress Beauvais (truncated because of Charles)

moments.quote: Some old people obviously live there & tend their gardens all summer.
The church is amazingly high – like our new convention centre.
we stood on the site of King Edward's windmill & looked down the hill
We drove home in the rain. A very good holiday.
